decisions.title: Phoenix Project V1 Goal: Working MVP
decisions.detail: The primary objective for Version 1 of the Phoenix Project is to deliver a working Minimum Viable Product (MVP).

evidence.quote: The main goal for V1 is a working MVP by September 1st.

decisions.title: V1 MVP Delivery Deadline
decisions.detail: The working MVP for V1 must be delivered by September 1st.

evidence.quote: a working MVP by September 1st.

decisions.title: Hard Deadline Constraint due to Marketing Campaign
decisions.detail: The September 1st deadline is a hard constraint, driven by a planned marketing campaign launch.

evidence.quote: We have a hard constraint on the deadline because of the marketing campaign launch on Sept 5th.

decisions.title: Marketing Campaign Launch Date
decisions.detail: A marketing campaign is scheduled to launch on September 5th.

evidence.quote: marketing campaign launch on Sept 5th.

decisions.title: Marketing Ads Booked
decisions.detail: The advertisements for the marketing campaign have already been booked.

evidence.quote: Yes, we've already booked the ads.

decisions.title: V1 User Authentication Requirement
decisions.detail: Version 1 of the product must include user authentication functionality.

evidence.quote: V1 must have user authentication

decisions.title: V1 Project Dashboard Requirement
decisions.detail: Version 1 of the product must include a working project dashboard.

evidence.quote: and a working project dashboard.

decisions.title: Performance Requirement: Super Fast
decisions.detail: The system must operate at a 'super fast' performance level.

evidence.quote: Let's make sure it's super fast.

decisions.title: Frontend Technology Decision: React
decisions.detail: React has been chosen as the frontend technology for the project.

evidence.quote: we are using React for the frontend

decisions.title: Backend Technology Decision: Python (FastAPI)
decisions.detail: Python with FastAPI has been chosen for the backend service, leveraging existing team expertise and libraries.

evidence.quote: Python (FastAPI) for the backend service.

decisions.title: Database Technology Decision: PostgreSQL
decisions.detail: PostgreSQL has been selected as the database technology.

evidence.quote: We'll use PostgreSQL.

decisions.title: Database Model: Standard Relational
decisions.detail: A standard relational model will be used for the database.

evidence.quote: Standard relational model is fine.

decisions.title: Open Question: Session Management Technology
decisions.detail: A question remains regarding whether to use Redis or standard database sessions for session management.

evidence.quote: Should we use Redis or just standard database sessions?

decisions.title: Session Management Decision: Standard PostgreSQL Sessions for V1
decisions.detail: For Version 1, standard PostgreSQL database sessions will be used for session management.

evidence.quote: Let's stick to standard postgres sessions for now

decisions.title: Redis Deferred to V2 (Unless Performance Blocker)
decisions.detail: The implementation of Redis for session management will be deferred to Version 2, unless performance issues in V1 necessitate its earlier adoption.

evidence.quote: Let's defer Redis to V2 unless it's a performance blocker.

decisions.title: Budget Constraint on Session Management
decisions.detail: The choice of standard PostgreSQL sessions for V1 is partly driven by a need to save budget.

evidence.quote: to save budget.

decisions.title: Constraint: Do Not Build Custom Authentication
decisions.detail: The team should not build a custom authentication system.

evidence.quote: We don't want to build it ourselves, right?

decisions.title: Authentication Provider Decision: Firebase Authentication for V1
decisions.detail: For Version 1, Firebase Authentication will be integrated as the authentication provider.

evidence.quote: for V1, let's just integrate Firebase Authentication.

decisions.title: Authentication Methods: Google Sign-in and Email Logins
decisions.detail: Firebase Authentication will be configured to support Google sign-in and email-based logins.

evidence.quote: We will configure it to allow Google sign-in and email logins.

decisions.title: Initial User Registration Fields
decisions.detail: Initially, user registration will collect email, full name, and role.

evidence.quote: Just email, full name, and role.

decisions.title: Excluded User Registration Fields
decisions.detail: Address and telephone number are not required for user registration at this time.

evidence.quote: We don't need address or telephone number for now.

decisions.title: Company Name Requirement for User Registration
decisions.detail: The company name must also be collected during user registration.

evidence.quote: we need to collect company name too!

decisions.title: Business Model: B2B
decisions.detail: The project serves a Business-to-Business (B2B) market.

evidence.quote: We are B2B.

decisions.title: Confirmation: Company Name Required at Signup
decisions.detail: It is confirmed that the company name is a mandatory field during the signup process.

evidence.quote: Company name is required during signup.

decisions.title: Final User Registration Fields Confirmed
decisions.detail: The confirmed user registration fields are Email, Full Name, Role, and Company Name.

evidence.quote: Got it. Email, full name, role, and company name.

decisions.title: Backend Deployment Platform Decision: Cloud Run
decisions.detail: Cloud Run is chosen for deploying the FastAPI backend application.

evidence.quote: I was thinking Cloud Run for the FastAPI app
Yes, we will place the database in a private VPC and connect Cloud Run via Serverless VPC Access.

decisions.title: Frontend Deployment Platform Decision: Vercel
decisions.detail: Vercel is chosen for deploying the React frontend application.

evidence.quote: Vercel for the React app.
Vercel is fine

decisions.title: Frontend Deployment Platform Approved: Vercel
decisions.detail: Vercel has been approved as the deployment platform for the React frontend.

evidence.quote: Vercel is fine

decisions.title: Open Question: Cloud Run Security
decisions.detail: A question was raised regarding the security of Cloud Run for backend deployment.

evidence.quote: is Cloud Run secure?

decisions.title: Database Private VPC Placement
decisions.detail: The database will be placed within a private Virtual Private Cloud (VPC).

evidence.quote: we will place the database in a private VPC

decisions.title: Cloud Run Connection via Serverless VPC Access
decisions.detail: Cloud Run will connect to the private VPC via Serverless VPC Access.

evidence.quote: connect Cloud Run via Serverless VPC Access.

decisions.title: Database Not Exposed to Public Internet
decisions.detail: The PostgreSQL instance must not be exposed to the public internet.

evidence.quote: the postgres instance is not exposed to the public internet.

decisions.title: Strict Technical Constraint for Security
decisions.detail: The security measures concerning database exposure and VPC connectivity are considered a strict technical constraint.

evidence.quote: This is a strict technical constraint for security.

decisions.title: Auth Flow Thorough Testing Requirement
decisions.detail: The authentication flow must be thoroughly tested.

evidence.quote: We need to test the auth flow thoroughly.

decisions.title: Open Question: Session Duration (Token Expiration)
decisions.detail: A question was raised about how long user sessions should last.

evidence.quote: how long does the session last?

decisions.title: Token Expiration Decision: 24 Hours
decisions.detail: Token expiration will initially be set to 24 hours, with the possibility for future adjustment.

evidence.quote: Let's set token expiration to 24 hours.

decisions.title: Dashboard Required Metrics
decisions.detail: The project dashboard must display active user metrics, system status, and project lists.

evidence.quote: We need active user metrics, system status, and project lists.

decisions.title: Decision to Defer Dashboard Feature Detailing
decisions.detail: Detailed discussion and definition of dashboard features will be deferred to the next sync meeting due to time constraints.

evidence.quote: Let's detail the dashboard features in the next sync.

decisions.title: Open Question: Who Handles Firebase Provisioning?
decisions.detail: The responsibility for handling Firebase provisioning is an open question.

evidence.quote: who is handling the Firebase provisioning?

decisions.title: Firebase Config Keys Deadline: Next Week
decisions.detail: The configuration keys for Firebase are needed by next week.

evidence.quote: I need the config keys by next week.

decisions.title: Firebase Provisioning Assigned to Mike
decisions.detail: Mike has been assigned the task of handling Firebase provisioning.

evidence.quote: I'll assign that to Mike.

decisions.title: Firebase Config Keys Deadline: July 8th
decisions.detail: Mike is expected to have the Firebase config keys ready by July 8th.

evidence.quote: He should have it ready by July 8th.

decisions.title: Dependency: Sync with Marketing on Dashboard Design
decisions.detail: There is a dependency to sync with the marketing team regarding the dashboard design next Wednesday.

evidence.quote: Let's make sure we sync with the marketing team on the dashboard design next Wednesday.

implementation_scope: Develop user authentication functionality leveraging Firebase Authentication for V1.
Configure Firebase Authentication for Google sign-in and email-based logins.
Implement user registration flow to collect Email, Full Name, Role, and Company Name.
Build a working project dashboard component.
Dashboard must display active user metrics, system status, and project lists.
Implement session management using standard PostgreSQL database sessions for V1.
Set token expiration for user sessions to 24 hours.
Develop a backend service using Python with FastAPI.
Develop a frontend application using React.
Deploy the FastAPI backend application to Cloud Run.
Deploy the React frontend application to Vercel.
Integrate Cloud Run with the private VPC via Serverless VPC Access to connect to the database.
technical_constraints: V1 MVP delivery by September 1st (hard deadline).
System must operate at a 'super fast' performance level.
Redis for session management is deferred to V2, unless performance issues arise in V1.
Do not build a custom authentication system.
Authentication provider for V1: Firebase Authentication.
Database must be placed within a private Virtual Private Cloud (VPC).
PostgreSQL instance must not be exposed to the public internet (strict security constraint).
Firebase config keys are required by July 8th.
open_technical_questions: Is Cloud Run secure for backend deployment?

acceptance_criteria: Given a user navigates to the registration page, When they provide a valid Email, Full Name, Role, and Company Name, Then they SHALL be able to successfully register.
Given a registered user, When they attempt to log in using their registered email and password, Then they SHALL be successfully authenticated and redirected to the project dashboard.
Given a user, When they attempt to log in using Google Sign-in, Then they SHALL be successfully authenticated and redirected to the project dashboard.
Given a user, When their session token has expired after 24 hours, Then they SHALL be prompted to log in again upon attempting to access protected resources.
The user registration process SHALL NOT require input fields for 'Address' or 'Telephone Number'.
Given a successfully logged-in user, When they access the project dashboard, Then the dashboard SHALL display active user metrics.
Given a successfully logged-in user, When they access the project dashboard, Then the dashboard SHALL display system status information.
Given a successfully logged-in user, When they access the project dashboard, Then the dashboard SHALL display a list of projects.
The system SHALL operate at a high performance level, perceived as 'super fast' by end-users.
The PostgreSQL database instance SHALL NOT be directly accessible from the public internet.
The Cloud Run backend application SHALL be able to establish a secure connection to the PostgreSQL database via Serverless VPC Access.
test_scenarios: Verify a new user can successfully register using a valid email, full name, 'Admin' role, and 'Test Company' company name.
Verify a new user can successfully register using Google sign-in and their profile information is captured.
Verify attempting to register with one or more of Email, Full Name, Role, or Company Name fields empty results in an appropriate error message.
Verify attempting to register with an invalid email format (e.g., 'test@.com') results in an appropriate error message.
Verify a registered user can successfully log in with correct email and password.
Verify a registered user can successfully log in using Google sign-in.
Verify attempting to log in with incorrect email or password results in an appropriate error message.
Verify that after a user's session token expires (24 hours), they are logged out or prompted to re-authenticate when trying to access protected dashboard content.
Verify a logged-in user can navigate to and view the project dashboard.
Verify an unauthenticated user attempting to access the dashboard is redirected to the login/registration page.
Verify direct connection attempts to the PostgreSQL database from a public IP address are rejected.
Verify the Cloud Run backend successfully connects to and queries the private PostgreSQL database.
edge_cases: Test user registration and login fields (Full Name, Role, Company Name, Email) with maximum allowed character lengths.
Test user registration and login fields with various special characters to ensure proper handling and prevent injection vulnerabilities.
Verify system behavior when a single user account logs in concurrently from multiple different devices or browsers.
Attempt to create a user account with an email address that already exists within the system.
Simulate high concurrent user load to evaluate if the system maintains 'super fast' performance and if PostgreSQL sessions become a performance blocker.
Attempt to access Cloud Run backend endpoints with malformed, expired, or missing authentication tokens.
unresolved_behaviors: The specific definition and calculation criteria for 'active user metrics' to be displayed on the dashboard are not yet defined.
The specific definition and content of 'system status' information to be displayed on the dashboard are not yet defined.
The specific definition and content of 'project lists' to be displayed on the dashboard are not yet defined.
Quantifiable performance metrics for 'super fast' operation (e.g., specific page load times, API response times, number of concurrent users supported) are not defined.
The comprehensive security implications and specific measures for Cloud Run beyond its connection to a private VPC are an open question.
The exact behavior when a user's token expires while they are actively interacting with the system (e.g., midway through a form submission or data entry) needs clarification.

decision_history: The primary objective for Version 1 of the Phoenix Project is to deliver a working Minimum Viable Product (MVP) by September 1st.
The September 1st delivery deadline is a hard constraint due to a planned marketing campaign launching on September 5th, for which ads are already booked.
Version 1 of the product must include user authentication functionality and a working project dashboard.
The system must operate at a 'super fast' performance level.
React has been chosen as the frontend technology, to be deployed on Vercel.
Python with FastAPI has been chosen for the backend service, leveraging existing team expertise and libraries, to be deployed on Cloud Run.
PostgreSQL has been selected as the database technology, using a standard relational model.

For Version 1, standard PostgreSQL database sessions will be used for session management to save budget. Redis implementation is deferred to Version 2 unless performance issues necessitate earlier adoption.
The team will not build a custom authentication system. Firebase Authentication will be integrated for V1, configured to allow Google sign-in and email-based logins.
Initial user registration fields will collect Email, Full Name, Role, and Company Name. Address and telephone number are explicitly not required for now, reflecting the B2B business model.

The database will be placed within a private Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) and Cloud Run will connect via Serverless VPC Access. The PostgreSQL instance must not be exposed to the public internet, which is a strict technical constraint for security.
The authentication flow must be thoroughly tested.
Token expiration will initially be set to 24 hours, with the possibility for future adjustment.
The project dashboard must display active user metrics, system status, and project lists. Detailed discussion and definition of dashboard features will be deferred to the next sync meeting.
Mike has been assigned the task of handling Firebase provisioning and is expected to have the config keys ready by July 8th.
dependencies: V1 MVP delivery by September 1st is critically dependent on a marketing campaign launch on September 5th, for which ads are already booked.
Development of Firebase integration is dependent on Mike provisioning Firebase and providing necessary configuration keys by July 8th.
Dashboard feature detailing and design are dependent on a sync with the marketing team next Wednesday.
Cloud Run's connection to the private VPC requires Serverless VPC Access (inferred from deployment architecture).
risks: Performance Risk (High Likelihood, Medium Impact): Deferring Redis for session management to save budget, while requiring a 'super fast' system, poses a risk that V1 performance may not meet expectations, potentially leading to rework or delays if Redis adoption becomes necessary sooner.

Deadline Miss Risk (Medium Likelihood, High Impact): The September 1st MVP deadline is a hard constraint tied to a marketing campaign. Any unforeseen technical challenges, scope creep, or delays in dependencies (e.g., Firebase provisioning, dashboard feature definition) could jeopardize this critical delivery date and impact the marketing launch.

Security Assurance Risk (Medium Likelihood, High Impact): While specific security measures for database isolation are defined (private VPC, Serverless VPC Access), the explicit open question regarding 'is Cloud Run secure?' suggests a potential unaddressed concern. If fundamental security assumptions are not fully satisfied, this could lead to vulnerabilities or significant rework of the backend infrastructure.
alignment_gaps: Performance Expectation vs. Budget/Scope: There's a potential gap in understanding or agreement on what 'super fast' performance (D008) precisely means, especially when contrasted with the budget-driven decision to defer Redis for session management (D015, D016). This could lead to differing interpretations of success criteria for V1 performance.

Cloud Run Security Assurance: Dave's direct question 'is Cloud Run secure?' (D029) indicates that not all stakeholders may be fully assured or clear on the security posture of the chosen backend deployment platform, despite the architectural decisions made for database isolation. This needs further clarification and assurance to prevent future concerns.
open_questions: Cloud Run Security (High Impact): 'Is Cloud Run secure?' (D029) - This fundamental security question about the backend deployment platform needs a clear, comprehensive answer and potentially a security review or detailed explanation to ensure all stakeholders are confident in the chosen architecture before significant development proceeds.

stakeholder.business_summary: The team confirmed the primary goal is to deliver a working product by September 1st to support a critical marketing campaign launching soon after. Key initial features will include user authentication and a basic project dashboard, with an emphasis on performance and budget-conscious choices. The database will be securely isolated.
key_impact: The product launch is synchronized with a pre-booked marketing campaign, maximizing initial market impact.
Users will have a secure sign-up and login process, and their data will be protected in an isolated database environment.
The initial project dashboard will provide essential business metrics like active users and project status.
Leveraging existing services for authentication and deferring advanced features helps manage development costs for the initial release.
decisions_needing_alignment: Finalizing the detailed features and design for the project dashboard, which requires input from the marketing team by next Wednesday.
Confirmation is needed regarding the security robustness of the Cloud Run platform chosen for backend deployment.
major_unresolved_questions: Is the chosen backend deployment platform, Cloud Run, sufficiently secure to meet our business standards?

issues.description: The phrasing for the dependency regarding the V1 MVP delivery and the marketing campaign might be slightly misconstrued. It states, 'V1 MVP delivery by September 1st is critically dependent on a marketing campaign launch on September 5th'. While the deadline is indeed *driven* by the marketing campaign, the delivery itself is not 'dependent' on the launch in the sense that the launch must occur first. Rather, the deadline is a *constraint* because of the upcoming launch.
issues.suggestion: Rephrase to clarify that the September 1st deadline is a hard constraint *due to* the marketing campaign launch on September 5th, rather than the MVP delivery being dependent on the launch event itself. For example: 'The September 1st MVP delivery deadline is a hard constraint driven by the marketing campaign launching on September 5th, for which ads are already booked.'

issues.description: The dependency 'Cloud Run's connection to the private VPC requires Serverless VPC Access (inferred from deployment architecture)' uses 'inferred'. Decision D031 explicitly states 'Cloud Run will connect to the private VPC via Serverless VPC Access.', making it a confirmed technical constraint rather than an inference.
issues.suggestion: Adjust the phrasing to reflect that this is a confirmed technical constraint or part of the defined architecture rather than an inference. For example: 'Cloud Run's connection to the private VPC is established via Serverless VPC Access, as defined in the deployment architecture.'

review.reviewer_notes: All role-specific outputs are well-structured, comprehensive, and traceable to the extracted decisions. No hallucinations or critical quality issues were found. Minor phrasing improvements are suggested for the PM view's dependencies for enhanced clarity and precision.
